data.title: Germany Warns EU Methane Rules Could Hinder LNG Imports Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Germany has expressed concerns that the European Union's regulations aimed at reducing methane emissions from oil and gas imports could obstruct essential liquefied natural gas (LNG) purchases. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Germany's efforts to diversify its energy suppliers and mitigate the impact of the ongoing Middle East conflict on energy prices. The warning comes as Germany seeks to secure stable energy sources in the face of geopolitical tensions that threaten to disrupt supply chains and elevate costs. The EU's methane regulations are part of broader environmental initiatives, but Germany argues that these measures could inadvertently affect its energy security strategy.

data.title: Calumet Shares Surge 14% Following Price Target Increase
data.description: Calumet shares experienced a significant rise of up to 14% on Monday after H.C. Wainwright increased its price target from $33 to $60 and maintained a Buy rating. According to NS3.AI, the investment firm attributed this optimistic outlook to stronger renewable fuels pricing and increasing demand. Additionally, Calumet began the week with a strengthened balance sheet, having refinanced its debt.

data.title: UK Prime Minister Starmer Announces 35-Nation Alliance for De-escalation Efforts
data.description: UK Prime Minister Starmer has announced the formation of a coalition comprising 35 countries aimed at de-escalating global tensions. According to PANews, this alliance seeks to address and mitigate ongoing international conflicts through diplomatic and cooperative measures. The initiative underscores the importance of multilateral efforts in maintaining global peace and stability.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Promises Swift Response to Iran's Attack on Israeli Refinery
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced in a media interview on March 30 that a response to Iran's attack on an Israeli refinery in Haifa will come "very soon." According to Odaily, Israeli reports on the same day confirmed that a refinery in the northern city of Haifa caught fire following a missile attack.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Whale Alert has reported the minting of 250 million USDC at the USDC Treasury. This development is significant as it may impact the liquidity and market dynamics of the stablecoin. The minting of such a large amount of USDC could influence trading activities and investor sentiment in the cryptocurrency market. Observers are closely monitoring the situation to assess its potential effects on market stability and capital flows.

data.title: Exemption Considered for Gulf of Mexico Oil and Gas Production
data.description: The "God Squad" is evaluating a potential exemption for oil and gas production in the Gulf of Mexico, which could bypass existing environmental protections. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing discussions surrounding this controversial measure. The exemption, if granted, would enable energy companies to continue operations without adhering to certain environmental regulations, sparking debate among environmentalists and industry stakeholders. The decision is expected to have significant implications for both the energy sector and environmental conservation efforts in the region.

data.title: Trump said a response to Iran's attack on an Israeli oil refinery would be "coming soon."
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that on March 30th local time, US President Trump stated in a media interview that his response to Iran's attack on an oil refinery in Haifa, Israel, would "come very soon." Israeli media reported on the 30th that the oil refinery in the northern Israeli city of Haifa caught fire during the missile attack. (CCTV News)

data.title: Sale of Seized Oil Tanker Faces Legal Challenge
data.description: The sale of a crude oil tanker, seized by the Trump administration in December, has encountered a legal obstacle. Bloomberg posted on X, reporting that the vessel's owner has taken steps to prevent the sale. The tanker, which was confiscated as part of a broader enforcement action, is now at the center of a legal dispute. The owner is challenging the sale, arguing that the seizure was unjustified. This development adds a new layer of complexity to the case, which has already drawn significant attention due to its international implications. The outcome of this legal battle could have broader ramifications for similar enforcement actions in the future.

data.title: SEC's Crypto Guidance Leaves Key Regulatory Standards Unclear, Say Lawyers
data.description: Lawyers from Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher have expressed concerns over the latest joint crypto guidance issued by the SEC. According to NS3.AI, while the guidance marks an improvement over previous language, it still fails to clarify essential regulatory standards. The lawyers highlighted that the guidance does not adequately define the contractual obligations required under the Howey Test. Additionally, it does not clearly align with the standards set by the Ripple case for secondary market token trades.

data.title: Bitcoin's Rally Faces Challenges as Smaller Investors Sell
data.description: Bitcoin's recent rebound towards $76,000 has seen smaller investors starting to sell, according to Glassnode. The firm noted that for the rally to be sustainable, it requires buying pressure from wallets of all sizes. Without this broader participation, the upward momentum may be limited.

data.title: Swift Advances Blockchain-Based Ledger for Cross-Border Payments
data.description: Swift has announced significant progress in its blockchain-based shared ledger initiative. According to Foresight News, after completing the design phase with a global banking group, Swift is now developing the first MVP iteration of the ledger. This system aims to enable interoperability between tokenized bank deposits and support 24-hour cross-border payments, with plans to commence real transactions this year. The ledger will utilize existing compliance processes and offer multiple settlement options, aiming to enhance payment execution speed, liquidity transparency, and reduce reconciliation workload between institutions.

data.title: Chainalysis Report Highlights Cryptocurrency Use in Drone Procurement
data.description:
Chainalysis has released a report indicating that low-cost commercial drones have become central tools in modern conflicts. According to Foresight News, organizations linked to Russia and Iran are increasingly using cryptocurrency to fund the purchase of low-cost military drones and components. Since 2022, pro-Russian volunteers and paramilitary groups have raised over $8.3 million in cryptocurrency donations through various blockchains.

The report highlights that sanctioned Russian drone developer KB Vostok's "Scalpel" drone is priced at approximately $2,200, with on-chain transactions showing multiple payments matching this price and its multiples. Additionally, these groups are shifting from Bitcoin to stablecoins for procurement to leverage their price stability. In Iran, the Ministry of Defense Export Center (Mindex) has openly promoted accepting cryptocurrency payments for advanced weapons, including Shahed drones and ballistic missiles. Chainalysis has detected a wallet used for purchasing drone components with direct or indirect financial links to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the Nobitex exchange. Data shows a significant increase in on-chain funds to related drone suppliers during a 12-day war between Iran and Israel in June 2025.

data.title: Circle Issues 250 Million USDC on Solana Network
data.description: Circle has issued 250 million USDC on the Solana network, according to BlockBeats. The transaction was detected by Whale Alert on March 30 at 23:26 UTC+8.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Comments on Energy Shock Uncertainty
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has expressed uncertainty regarding the extent of the energy shock's impact. According to PANews, Powell stated that it is too early to draw conclusions about the situation.

data.title: U.S. Labor Department Proposes New Rule on Fiduciary Duties for Investment Options
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Labor has released a proposed rule document outlining new guidelines for fiduciary duties in selecting designated investment alternatives. According to PANews, the Employee Benefits Security Administration (EBSA) under the Labor Department has introduced a draft regulation titled 'Fiduciary Duties In Selecting Designated Investment Alternatives.' This proposal aims to clarify and provide a safe harbor for fiduciaries under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) when choosing investment options for participant-directed individual account plans, such as 401(k) plans.

The draft specifies that ERISA remains neutral regarding the types of investments. Fiduciaries can include asset allocation funds with alternative assets such as private equity, hedge funds, real estate, digital assets, commodities, infrastructure, and lifetime income strategies, provided they adhere to prudent procedures. The proposal introduces six key considerations: performance, fees, liquidity, valuation, benchmarks, and complexity. It emphasizes that courts should give a high degree of judicial respect to fiduciary judgments when the safe harbor procedures are followed, aiming to reduce litigation risks and encourage the inclusion of alternative assets in plans under reasonable conditions.

data.title: Powell: It's too early to draw conclusions now, as we don't know how big the energy shock will be.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: We don't know how big the energy shock will be, and it's too early to draw conclusions. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Trades Near $70,000 Amid U.S.-Iran Discussions
data.description: Bitcoin is trading close to $70,000 following remarks by U.S. President Donald Trump about ongoing discussions with Iran regarding a new leadership structure. According to NS3.AI, this development is contributing to a broader trading range of $65,000–$70,000. The market is experiencing reduced volatility due to continued covered call selling and market-maker hedging activities.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Monitors Private Sector Concerns
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated on Monday that the Fed is closely monitoring potential issues within the private sector. According to PANews, Powell remarked at Harvard University that private credit constitutes a relatively small portion of a vast asset system, and the Fed is paying close attention to this area. He did not specify the current level of risk in the sector. Powell added that dismissing concerns entirely would be ominous.

Powell emphasized that there is currently no evidence of contagious risk in private credit, and conditions do not support the development of a broader systemic event. He noted that private credit is undergoing adjustments and does not see any connections between private credit and banks.

data.title: Market Pricing Indicates Shift from Fed Rate Hike to Possible Rate Cut
data.description: Market pricing has shifted, indicating a withdrawal of bets on a Federal Reserve rate hike, with a focus now on the possibility of a rate cut this year. According to Odaily, this change reflects evolving expectations in the financial markets. The adjustment in market sentiment suggests that investors are reconsidering the likelihood of monetary policy easing by the Federal Reserve.

data.title: U.S. Stock Indexes Rise Following Fed Chair Powell's Remarks
data.description: U.S. stock indexes experienced a slight increase after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell delivered a speech. According to ChainCatcher, Powell's comments contributed to the expansion of gains in the market. The reaction reflects investor sentiment following the Fed Chair's address, which often influences market movements.
